blockingIssues.description: The generated cron line starts with `CRON_TZ=...` on the same line as the schedule, so cron parses it as an environment assignment instead of a runnable job.
The generated cron command invokes `scripts/run_daily.sh` directly even though the file is tracked non-executable, so Unix cron runs can fail with `Permission denied`.

`tests/test_data_integrity.py` validates a manufactured parquet fixture instead of the real training-data builder or cached training-data path, so production regressions can slip through while the tests stay green.
`tests/test_antileak.py` also exercises only the synthetic fixture and a timestamp-only assertion, so it cannot catch leakage in the real training-data feature values such as same-day contamination.
CLV sync selects the latest snapshot by `(game_pk, market_type)` only, but bet performance does not store `book_name`, so closing lines can come from the wrong sportsbook.
CLV is only synced once during settlement and does not refresh automatically when a later closing snapshot arrives, so normal runs can leave stale or zero CLV values unless callers manually resync.

The pipeline passes already-retrying fetch helpers into `CircuitBreaker.call()`, so the breaker opens only after five exhausted retry batches instead of the required five consecutive upstream failures.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Windows-specific startup guidance telling workers on win32 to prefer the repository's existing Windows manifest commands and direct interpreter invocations over `bash .factory/init.sh` when bootstrapping or validating this repo.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t27-scheduler-setup` found the worker deviated from the base startup procedure because `.factory/init.sh` is unreliable on Windows, and `.factory/library/environment.md` already documents that the manifest's Windows commands are the working path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an automation-testing branch or companion procedure for pytest-only validation features so workers are not forced through a pipeline-runtime implementation checklist when the assignment is strictly test-suite work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t28-data-integrity-tests` found the current `pipeline-worker` skill remained implementation/runtime oriented while the assigned feature only added regression tests; the worker handoff recorded a justified procedure deviation for that mismatch.

rejectedObservations.observation: Fix `.factory/services.yaml` `commands.init_windows` to remove the doubled single quotes around `data/mlb.db`.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented and non-additive: `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` already records the Windows quoting bug, and correcting it would require overwriting an existing services manifest entry outside the additive-only scrutiny-update scope.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document or add a shared command for the close-line capture step required by CLV.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the correct operational shape is unresolved until the t30 blocking issues are fixed, because current CLV behavior lacks bookmaker scoping and does not define when the authoritative closing snapshot should be captured.
